library_preparation_preflight.summary: No extra dependency, Docker image, environment variable, or system property is needed. The 2.0.0 artifact already brings its required runtime dependencies, and the javac failure is caused by Maven SCM 2.x API changes: org.apache.maven.scm.log.DefaultLog is gone and GitRemoteInfoCommand.createCommandLine is no longer static.
library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: Adapt tests to the Maven SCM 2.0.0 public API instead of adding old 1.x classes. Use the logger-free GitStatusConsumer and GitRemoteInfoConsumer constructors, and instantiate GitRemoteInfoCommand with its required settings map before calling createCommandLine. If exercising real git command execution rather than command-line construction or consumers, create an isolated temporary Git repository and local test remote in test code; no network remote should be required.
risks: Adding maven-scm-api 1.x only to recover DefaultLog would mix incompatible Maven SCM API versions and could hide the real 2.0.0 compatibility issue.

Tests that execute actual commit/tag operations may need local git user.name and user.email configured inside the temporary repository, but the existing compile failure does not indicate a deterministic setup dependency.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8307 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.scm:maven-scm-provider-gitexe:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015
